manual_intro: Canberra is one of the strongest snow-guide cities because it has nearby snow play plus direct access to the Snowy Mountains. This guide starts local, then moves into the bigger NSW resort network.
place_description: Canberra-region snow-play and beginner-ski site where machine-made snow improves first-snow reliability. Best time: June to September, with July and August best and road or operating updates checked first. Book/check sessions and operating conditions before travelling.

manual_intro: Canberra is one of the strongest snow-guide cities because it has nearby snow play plus direct access to the Snowy Mountains. This guide starts local, then moves into the bigger NSW resort network.
place_description: NSW's largest resort hub and a high-confidence anchor for resort skiing, lessons and family snow trips. Best time: the June to October snow season, with July and August usually the strongest snow window. Use current resort, road and Skitube information before travelling.

place_description: Australia's highest resort village and a deep-winter Snowy Mountains classic. Best time: the June to October snow season, with July and August usually the strongest snow window. Winter access is by oversnow transport from Perisher.

manual_intro: Canberra is one of the strongest snow-guide cities because it has nearby snow play plus direct access to the Snowy Mountains. This guide starts local, then moves into the bigger NSW resort network.
place_description: ACT high-country snow-gum area for natural snow walks and winter scenery. Best time: after winter cold snaps or fresh snow reports; snow is occasional and not guaranteed. Road closures, chains and alpine weather can all affect access.

manual_intro: Canberra is one of the strongest snow-guide cities because it has nearby snow play plus direct access to the Snowy Mountains. This guide starts local, then moves into the bigger NSW resort network.
place_description: Classic ACT range snow-play fallback when conditions line up around the Brindabellas and Mt Franklin Road. Best time: after winter cold snaps or fresh snow reports; snow is occasional and not guaranteed. Only worthwhile after snow reports and safe-road confirmation.
